In this, I will right-click and open the VS Code. And I will create a file, index.html, okay? Now, I have created an index.html file here. Now, I want to tell you something. There are many things in HTML that you have to explore by yourself. I will tell you some things here. And after this video, I will conclude HTML. If, for example, in HTML, you want me to toggle the screencast mode by turning on the word wrap. Suppose, you want to write this in HTML. 3 is greater than 4, okay? Suppose, you want to write this. If you try to write this in HTML, let me show you its preview. So, it has written 3 is greater than 4 here, okay? Now, suppose, I write here, this is the code for paragraph, okay? And I write something like this. Now, I have written this here, okay? I want it to appear here. But you see here, it won't appear. Why not? Because how will it know that I want to write the paragraph code or that I actually want to use this p tag as a syntax. So, HTML entities are to be avoided from this thing. Now, let me tell you what HTML entities are. If you write amp%lt, lt for less than, then you can write this as lt semicolon. Now, if you write amp%lt semicolon here, and if you replace it with amp%lt semicolon, and you replace this with greater than simple, you will replace it with amp%lt semicolon. And if you replace it with amp%lt semicolon, then you will write amp%lt semicolon. M%GT semicolon, I'll show you by doing it. If you replace it with M%GT semicolon, then what will happen? That it will appear here as it is written. As you can see, I'll zoom in and show you. So what happens is that HTML entities are used to write those things which may collide with HTML syntax. What does it mean to collide? If we write the paragraph tag code here, then how will HTML know that this is the paragraph tag code? Or do you actually want to write this thing as it is? There's a pre-tag for this too. If you put anything in the pre-tag, and you write p, this is a paragraph, if you write it like this, then it will appear as it is here, as you can see. It will appear as it is, and along with that, if you write new lines here, this is after few new lines, and some more. If you write it like this, then it will appear as it is, keeping all the spaces intact. Keeping all the new lines intact. But it's not like a tag will show up here. For tags, we'll have to use entities only. So this happens in pre-tag HTML. So we've seen entities, we've seen pre-tag. If you want to put a copyright symbol, then you can write ampersand copy semicolon. And as you write ampersand copy semicolon, then you'll get to see the copyright symbol here, as you can see here. So you can do things like this with the help of HTML entities.
